extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won from 25th position with 800m to go after Quick-Step suffered positioning crisis, while DENNIS Rohan took pink via intermediate bonus seconds
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan hunted intermediate bonus seconds specifically to claim pink, showing GC teams target sprints strategically pre-TTT
Quick-Step positioning crisis at 800m forced VIVIANI Elia to advance from 25th, yet still won—indicates superior power overcomes poor positioning
BENNETT Sam's team explicitly refused leadout support, revealing Bora prioritized GC over sprint stages early in Grand Tours
Spectator wall allegedly assisted breakaway rider at climb summit, suggesting Israeli stages had unusual crowd interference affecting tactics

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: won from catastrophic 25th position at 800m, demonstrating exceptional acceleration capacity when leadout fails
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: aggressively contested intermediate sprints for bonus seconds to take pink, showing GC awareness before crucial TTT
rider_signals.MARECZKO Jakub: positive: second place suggests stronger positioning discipline than VIVIANI despite losing sprint
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: third place but team openly refused dedicated leadout, indicating Bora had alternative stage race priorities
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: neutral: challenged DENNIS Rohan for intermediate bonuses, revealing time trialists prioritizing seconds before ITT stages
